St. Denis helped shape modern dance, inspiring many with her graceful movements and creative ideas.
activity_question: What special dance performance did Ruth St. Denis create?
follow_up_question: How do you think a dance about the five senses might look?

title: A Naval Hero's Birth
story: A future hero of the seas entered the world on January 20, 1883. Bertram Home Ramsay was born in London, England. He grew up to become an important naval officer. As an Admiral, Ramsay led two crucial operations during World War II. He oversaw the daring Dunkirk evacuations and later planned the massive Normandy landings.
activity_question: What two important operations did Admiral Ramsay oversee?
activity_choices: Pearl Harbor attack and Battle of Midway
Submarine warfare and aircraft carrier missions
follow_up_question: Why do you think the Dunkirk evacuations were so important?

title: A Sci-Fi Star Is Born
story: A new star of science fiction lit up the world in 1884. On January 20, Abraham Merritt was born in Beverly, New Jersey. He grew up to become a famous magazine editor and author. Merritt's imagination soared with books like The Moon Pool, taking readers on wild adventures through strange new worlds.
activity_question: What type of stories did Abraham Merritt write?
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous science fiction author?

title: Little Orphan Annie's Creator Born
story: Harold Gray, the artistic genius behind the famous comic strip Little Orphan Annie, entered the world on January 20, 1894. Born in Kankakee, Illinois, Gray would go on to create a character that captured hearts for generations. His red-haired, spunky orphan girl first appeared in newspapers in 1924, becoming an instant hit.
activity_question: What famous comic strip did Harold Gray create?
bonus_fact: Little Orphan Annie inspired a popular Broadway musical and several movies.
